generated_oml:
instance PhotometricFixtureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Photometric sensor attached to each lighting fixture, providing illumination, colour‑temperature, and on/off status."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

instance LightTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "LightTwinComponent interprets BIM‑defined lighting fixtures and injects a UE5 light object into the scene."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

instance UnrealTwinController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "UnrealTwinController observes DittoDevice state changes and publishes updates via DittoConnection endpoints to drive the physical LED driver."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

instance PhotometricDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhotometricFixtureSensor
    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

    base:desc "Illumination level measured by the photometric sensor."

    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment

]

    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

]

    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

    base:isContainedIn BuildingTwinEnvironment
]

]

]

instance BuildingTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Root environment containing all twin components."
    base:contains PhotometricFixtureSensor, LightTwinComponent, UnrealTwinController, PhotometricDataStream, IlluminationMeasurement, ColorTemperatureMeasurement, OnOffStatusMeasurement, DittoDataStoreEnabler, DittoPolicyValidatorEnabler, HonoReceiverEnabler, MDETransformationEngineEnabler, RuntimeDeploymentEngineEnabler, DittoRuntimeStackEnabler, UE5ViewServerEnabler, RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService, DirectControlService, ImmersiveVisualizationService, RealTimeMonitoringInsight, PredictiveFaultInsight, EnergyOptimizationAction, ActuatorControlAction, ImmersiveDashboardAction, BIMGeometricModel, ControlLogicModel, DeviceMetadataModel, StructuredBIMData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework supports continuous real‑time data exchange, ensuring the digital representation stays synchronised with its physical counterpart in real time. The system provides bidirectional communication that enables up‑to‑date monitoring and control without explicit fixed sampling intervals in the document, indicating a design aimed at real‑time interaction with minimal latency."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellations are orchestrated through a modular, service‑oriented architecture centered on a DT Framework that serves as a central hub. The framework offers standardized extension points enabling the integration of custom services and analytics, while runtime configuration management allows adaptations without full redeployment. Component integration follows a service‑oriented pattern: Building Control Engineers use BIM tools to model devices and trigger deployment, DT Engineers extend the framework by plugging in specialized analytics or control services, and Facility Managers interact with the deployed DT for real‑time monitoring and control. Resource management is supported by a scalable broker within the framework, allowing scaling from component‑level to entire infrastructure systems. Maintainability is achieved via low‑overhead updates, and interoperability across heterogeneous systems is ensured through common data models and runtime agreements. Overall orchestration guarantees bidirectional continuous data exchange between the physical building and its digital twin, facilitating a holistic approach to building life‑cycle management."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle in this framework follows a model‑driven path that begins with BIM enrichment where Building Control Engineers annotate physical assets with explicit sensor, actuator, and controller semantics directly inside the BIM workspace. These enriched models are then subjected to model‑based engineering (MDE) transformations that map the BIM representation to a deployable semantic data model and automatically generate the corresponding DT artifacts (e.g., device configuration, connectivity adapters, and service descriptors). The conversion phase produces deployable artifacts that are staged for deployment to the runtime platform. Deployment, governed by the DT Engineer, respectively instantiates the digital twins and configures the real‑time data connectors so that every change in the physical building (via IoT gateways) is mirrored in the digital space. Throughout the operational phase, Facility Managers continuously monitor and interact with the twin through immersive visualization (Unreal Engine) and real‑time dashboards, while custom analytics plugins can be added at runtime via the framework’s standardized extension points. Lifecycle governance is explicit: Building Control Engineers own the modeling phase, DT Engineers oversee artifact generation and deployment, and Facility Managers drive operational interaction, ensuring traceability and a single source of truth."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured by validating the enriched BIM model against a custom schema before pipeline execution, guaranteeing that all required semantic annotations are present. Runtime fidelity is preserved through Ditto’s policy‑based validation, which enforces value ranges, units, and pre‑execution safety checks on actuator commands. Fault tolerance is achieved with Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once message delivery guaranteeing message receipt during network interruptions, and Ditto’s persistent event store that preserves state even during service disruptions. Quality assurance is performed via continuous monitoring of event logs, automated integrity checks, and reconciliation between physical timestamps and virtual twin states, ensuring consistent and trustworthy model behaviour."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Bidirectional data exchange facilitated by the DT framework, supporting interoperability across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external environments via a modular framework that exposes standardized extension points for service integration and supports bidirectional data exchange. While specific protocols are not detailed, the design emphasizes interoperability across heterogeneous systems, allowing the framework to serve as a bridge for external services, analytics, and enterprise systems within the AECO domain."
]
